text:
An animal on wheels!

Swiss-Mile recently launched the ANYmal with wheels on its feet that can do all kinds of amazing things.

Swiss-Mile, a spin-off of ETH Zurich’s Robotic Systems Lab, is commercializing robots that have both legs and wheels to perform a variety of tasks, including mapping, inspection, disaster relief, and logistics in urban environments. The robot is based on ETH’s ANYmal quadruped and can travel at speeds up to 22 kilometers per hour with a payload capacity up to 50 kg.

Exciting times ahead!

Ever imagined a robot riding a bike?

Well, it's not just a concept anymore! Check out this captivating video where PRIMER-V2, a Japanese robot built by Dr. Guero, is mastering the art of cycling. It shows that the world of robotics is getting more intriguing day by day as new inventions continue surprising people.

Exciting times ahead!

Meet Parkie, the world's first autonomous driving-based parking robot (AMR).

Developed by the Korean vehicle manufacturing company, HL Mando, it offers seamless parking convenience. Parkie not only enhances the overall value of buildings but also revolutionizes the utilization of parking lots.

Exciting times ahead!

Follow Abhisumat Kundu for more such content.

THIS IS INCREDIBLE!!!

The Tiny Surveyor is a small robotic device designed to autonomously pre-mark lines on the road before painting. It interfaces with GPS or other surveying equipment to receive precise location data.

Operators can upload digital files to the Tiny Surveyor via a USB connection. It then uses this information to navigate to the correct location and mark out the road lines.

Exciting times ahead!

Follow Abhisumat Kundu for more such content.

AR TECH GAMIFIES HOME CLEANING!!!

'Spatial Vacuuming' is a new mixed reality app from a solo developer designed to gamify home cleaning. The application uses the external cameras on an XR headset to scan the user's home floor layout, then overlays a sheet of color on the floor.

Once the user starts vacuuming, the color will disappear as the vacuum passes over it. This enables the player to clean their home in a fun and engaging way, as they must remove the entire sheet of color, meaning they must vacuum their whole floor to clear the game.

The application is barebones in its current state, offering only this one feature that colors the floor, serving more effectively as a proof-of-concept than a full-blown gamified cleaning application. However, even being just the essentials, Spatial Vacuuming is an excellent way of making home cleaning more engaging and visually rewarding.

Exciting times ahead!

Follow Abhisumat Kundu for more such content.

Fully automated AI restaurant opens in California!!!

Robots cook the burgers and deep fry the French fries at CaliExpress by Flippy, a new restaurant that opened in late December last year. People can watch robots prepare their meals from start to finish after they place an order. Orders can also be customised, a news release said.

The restaurant came to life, thanks to the partnership between Cali Group, a technology-forward holding company; Miso Robotics, the creator of Flippy, the world's first AI-powered robotic fry station; and PopID, a technology company that uses biometrics to simplify ordering and payment systems.

Exciting times ahead!

Now you can physically interact with someone at any part of the world!!! 😱😱

This is inFORM Tangible Media Interface. It is an innovative technology that enables users to interact with digital content through a dynamic, shape-shifting surface.

It utilizes a tangible interface, resembling a table with a responsive display that physically changes to represent 3D data.

Exciting times ahead!!

Follow Abhisumat Kundu for more such content.

AI POWERED MIND CONTROLLED PROSTHETIC ARMS!!!

Harnessing latent signals from arm nerves, mind-controlled prosthetics are enabling real-time, intuitive finger-level control of a robotic hand.

The approach involves taming nerve endings, separating nerve bundles, and amplifying signals using muscle grafts and machine learning algorithms. This not only provides individual finger control but also offers intuitive prosthetic control from the first try, eliminating the need for extensive learning by participants.

Kudos to the researchers for pushing the boundaries of neuroprosthetics and paving the way for a future where amputees can fully restore able-bodied hand movements.

Exciting times ahead!

Follow Abhisumat Kundu for such heartwarming content.

𝐓𝐡𝐢𝐬 𝐢𝐬 𝐠𝐫𝐨𝐮𝐧𝐝𝐛𝐫𝐞𝐚𝐤𝐢𝐧𝐠!!

Lenovo just launched the ThinkBook Transparent Display Laptop, unveiled at Mobile World Congress!

Featuring a 17.3" MicroLED display with up to 55% transparency and a dazzling 1000 nit peak brightness, this laptop blurs the lines between virtual and physical worlds. Perfect for digital artists and AR enthusiasts, its flat touch keyboard seamlessly transforms into a drawing tablet, offering a dynamic user experience.

Stay tuned as Lenovo explores AI integration to enhance user interaction. Witness the future of laptops unfold!

Exciting times ahead!

𝐓𝐞𝐬𝐥𝐚'𝐬 𝐍𝐞𝐱𝐭 𝐌𝐨𝐯𝐞 𝐖𝐢𝐥𝐥 𝐋𝐞𝐚𝐯𝐞 𝐘𝐨𝐮 𝐒𝐩𝐞𝐞𝐜𝐡𝐥𝐞𝐬𝐬!

The latest video shared by Tesla's Optimus account on X showcases the remarkable evolution of the Optimus Gen 2 robot. This newer build is not only 10kg lighter but also walks a staggering 30% faster than its predecessor.

In the footage, the humanoid robot can be seen confidently striding around Tesla's testing facility, with Tesla's Head of Optimus Engineering, Milan Kovac highlighting that the robot's walking speed has reached an impressive 0.6 meters per second (~1.34 mph), marking a significant 30% speed boost from previous versions. The ultimate goal is for Optimus to achieve speeds of up to five miles per hour.

This advancement in robotics technology opens up a world of possibilities for Tesla, with CEO Elon Musk previously expressing the potential for their robot business to surpass their vehicle division in significance.

Where do you envision Tesla's Optimus robot making the most impact? Share your thoughts and join the conversation below!

This Indian company is emerging as a formidable competitor to Tesla!

Swaayatt Robots is a pioneering company focused on developing autonomous driving technology for unstructured environments and challenging traffic conditions, particularly in India. Led by founder and CEO Sanjeev Sharma, the company's mission is to make connected autonomous driving technology accessible, affordable, and available to everyone.

Swaayatt Robots is dedicated to achieving level-5 autonomous driving, with a current goal of conducting a compelling 100 KM/H autonomous driving demonstration on Indian roads. The company's research heavily emphasizes decision-making and motion planning under uncertainty, with a significant focus on reinforcement learning.

Notably, Swaayatt Robots aims to demonstrate 100km/hr autonomous driving on Indian roads within eight to nine months, showcasing their advanced algorithms that enable safe and efficient navigation in complex environments.

Additionally, the company is actively involved in developing algorithms for multi-agent negotiation and intent analysis, essential for navigating dynamic and unpredictable traffic scenarios.

Exciting times ahead!

New version of Atlas robot! 😳

'We are unveiling the next generation of humanoid robots-a fully electric Atlas robot designed for real-world applications.

The new Atlas builds on decades of research and furthers our commitment to delivering the most capable, useful mobile robots solving the toughest challenges in industry today: with Spot, with Stretch, and now with Atlas.

Stay tuned to see what the world's most dynamic humanoid robot can really do-in the lab, in the factory, and in our lives.' said Boston Dynamics.

This is hilarious 😂!

A few days ago, L&T chairman SN Subrahmanyan asked his employees to work for 90 hours a week, and even on Sunday.

'How long can you stare at your wife? What do you do sitting at home? If I can make you work on Sundays, I will be happier. Because I work on Sunday also,' he said.

This statement has been deeply criticised by the Chairman of Mahindra Group, Anand Mahindra.

'If you're not spending time at home, with friends, or reading, how will you bring the right inputs to your decisions?' he questioned.

Follow Abhisumat Kundu for more such updates!

Medivis is redefining surgery forever!!

Now, a surgeon can wear an AR headset that superimposes data on a patient’s body during an operation, creating a visual guidance system that can assist with complicated procedures like brain-tumor removal, and reduce errors. It’s already in action at hospitals like Houston’s MD Anderson Cancer Center.

The company’s technology translates complex 2D medical imaging data, such as MRIs and CT scans, into a 3D visualization that surgeons can interact with. This 3D representation can then be superimposed or overlayed onto a patient’s body using commercially available AR headsets.

Medivis’ technology can be thought of as a ‘virtual GPS system’ for surgeons — it helps them navigate complex anatomical landscapes during operations and gives them a more accurate, intuitive understanding of the anatomy with which they’re dealing.

Exciting times ahead!
